considered_alternatives.reason: Independent review operationalization would build on strategic registries that still contained coherence drift.
Objective coverage hardening had lower immediate governance value than repairing authoritative strategic metadata.

rationale: Repair authoritative strategic registry coherence before extending independent review governance or opening lower-priority objective coverage work.
roadmap_debt: Objective coverage remains partial.
Strategic evidence quality remains weak.
Capability intelligence remains the dominant roadmap track.

considered_alternatives.reason: Deferred pending 66C completion; designated corrective path for objective coverage gaps per SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005.

rationale: Calibrate strategic review finding rules: SRS-RULE-007 recalibrated to fire only on active growth (not completed-track depth); SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005 suggested_resolution updated to name 64H as designated corrective path. Close BR-004 (independent_review_governance) at advisory level. Scope constrained per IRG challenge review: no workflow coupling, review remains advisory, no broader operationalization.
roadmap_debt: Broader independent review workflow operationalization not included (deferred per IRG challenge review constraints).
BR-004 advisory closure represented in governance_boundaries; registry status remains active until 66C completes.
Recommended next phase: 64H — Multi-Runtime Objective Coverage Hardening.

rationale: Strategic Review findings SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005 from 66C (per SRR-66B-001 authority) identified OBJ-003 primary_count=1 as a classification artifact from the 65D bulk mapping. 64H was the designated corrective path per SRS-RULE-004/005 suggested_resolution text. Cross-track movement from BR-004 closure (66C) to BR-001 resumption (64H) requires strategic lineage per the 65J model. IRG challenge review approved_with_changes; scope constrained to reclassification proposals + evidence reports; direct_map_mutation_allowed=False.
roadmap_debt: SRS-RULE-004 (partial completeness) not cleared by 64H; future primary-adding phases required.
SRS-RULE-006 (evidence quality) may improve only when evidence reports are consumed by strategic review model.
Reclassification proposals OCH-PROP-64H-001 through OCH-PROP-64H-005 require human approval before map update.

rationale: Recent phases (64H series) repeatedly surfaced post-‘implementation complete’ defects (stale lineage fixtures, task contract scope violations, evidence inconsistency, review supersession ambiguity), demonstrating that IRG review state should be automatically visible in the governed workflow rather than human-recall-dependent. The original automated-review-loop proposal was challenged twice by IRG self-review and reduced to minimum viable scope: surface the latest non-superseded review state in session bootstrap only, with review_blocks_any_operation=False. This extends BR-004 into a second arc (review loop visibility) per explicit human decision; no new branch created.
roadmap_debt: pcae check / task transition / phase handoff IRG surfacing deferred to a future phase decision.
Phase activation gate, lineage write gate, and roadmap mutation gate explicitly deferred.
Warning-fatigue mitigation (display-state suppression) deferred until multi-trigger surfacing exists.

rationale: Automatic visibility without workflow authority was explicitly approved for IRG Challenge. 66E extends BR-004 from bootstrap-only review visibility into deterministic, advisory-only challenge surfacing at summary-oriented lifecycle boundaries so assumptions, blind spots, inconsistencies, counterfactuals, and uncertainty are displayed without changing approvals, readiness, or command outcomes.
roadmap_debt: pcae check integration remains deferred to avoid hidden workflow authority.
No challenge persistence, lineage, override, acknowledgement, or remediation workflow is introduced in 66E.
Attention-fatigue refinement beyond bounded summary output remains future work.

rationale: 66E deterministic challenge engine was identified as generating the same questions every session regardless of governance evolution, undermining skepticism quality. 67A upgrades to comparative assessment (new/persistent/resolved), historical confidence model (high/medium/low epistemic reliability), contradiction synthesis via synthesis anchors and tension patterns, persistent-concern suppression after threshold phases, and differentiated surface strategy per lifecycle boundary. derived_history_is_historically_observed=False; confidence_modifies_attention_level=False.
roadmap_debt: Challenge persistence remains stateless; no archived challenge history introduced.
Resolved concern derivation is not implemented (stateless engine cannot derive resolutions).
Attention-level escalation with age explicitly rejected (regression_finding_implies_failure=False).

rationale: 67A identified that IRGC measures challenge generation but not challenge impact — it can classify new/persistent/resolved concerns but cannot detect whether challenge output appears correlated with governance evolution. 67B adds a non-binding correlation observation layer: domain-aligned artifact detection, temporal proximity signals, CorrelationPatterns (public), and calibration effects. InfluenceHypothesis is computed internally and never surfaced; humans see correlation, not influence. The principle: the engine may reason about influence internally, but output must remain correlation-framed. No persistence, no blocking, no governance authority.
roadmap_debt: Calibration effects (suppression_weight_delta) are computed but not applied — actual suppression modification deferred to a future phase.
Resolved concern derivation remains stateless (inherited from 67A).
Temporal proximity uses phase sort-key gap, not calendar time — coarse but deterministic.

rationale: 67B introduced a challenge impact assessment layer (correlation observations, calibration effects) but the IRGC engine still allocates attention uniformly across all findings regardless of informational value. 68A designs a deterministic, stateless attention allocation layer: AttentionSignals classify informational characteristics (novelty, contradiction, correlation strength, domain diversity); AttentionBudgets constrain per-surface output; AttentionDecisions distinguish surface from background without implying governance significance. visibility_is_importance=False; suppression_is_resolution=False; attention_allocation_affects_governance=False; no persistence, no blocking, no governance authority.
roadmap_debt: AttentionDecisions are computed but not yet applied to render_irg_challenge_compact_lines output — actual surface filtering deferred to a future phase.
Calibration effects from 67B remain unapplied; their interaction with attention allocation deferred.
Persistent attention memory explicitly rejected; future phases must re-evaluate if compelling evidence emerges.

rationale: 68A designed the adaptive attention allocation layer but left AttentionDecisions unconnected to the rendering path. 68B wires the allocator into automatic compact rendering surfaces (bootstrap, handoff, completion) via render_irg_challenge_compact_lines_with_allocation(). Explicit pcae irg-challenge command and JSON output retain all findings. allocator_changes_visibility_only=True; findings_unchanged_by_allocator=True; suppressed_findings_remain_available=True; attention_allocation_does_not_affect_command_outcomes=True. No governance outcomes are changed.

68C effectiveness review identified that persistent_background signal was computed but not actively used as a demotion criterion in _irca_allocate_attention() Step 3. Persistent findings competed equally with non-persistent findings during diversity_fill, reducing repetition reduction to incidental rather than structural. 68D adds competitive persistence demotion: Step 3 sort key becomes (is_persistent_background, domain, finding_id) so non-persistent findings precede persistent ones when budget is binding. Demotion is conditional — persistent findings surface unchanged when no non-persistent alternatives compete. demotion_is_scheduling_not_priority_ranking=True. persistence_implies_lower_priority=False maintained. Completion surface bypass documented as intentional via _IRCA_COMPLETION_SURFACE_NOTE. Rejected: hard demotion (makes absolute governance claim), weight-based scoring (implies importance ordering), rotational visibility (requires persistence). This closes the structural gap identified in 68C and completes the BR-004 attention arc.
